page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. This server features 4 vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, delivering a dedicated CPU allocation. It is configured with 32 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. The underlying x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16, and cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% in stress-ng tests, alongside average single-core Geekbench and text processing results. However, multi-core scaling is limited, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and software compilation benchmarks falling into the bottom 25%. Memory subsystem performance is a notable bottleneck; while cached small-block read bandwidth is weak, large-block uncached read and write memory bandwidth benchmarks register in the bottom 10%.

Given its high memory density relative to compute resources, the c3d-highmem-4 offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require intense parallel processing. It is well-suited for small-scale database operations, caching layers, and lightweight text or language model inference tasks using smaller models. However, it is less optimal for heavy multi-threaded applications, large-scale data compilation, or high-throughput web serving due to its constrained multi-core performance and uncached memory bandwidth limitations.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 vCPUs and 32 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it allocates 2 physical cores with hyper-threading and supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instruction sets. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU performance, though multi-core scaling and uncached memory bandwidth are relatively weak. This resource profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand heavy parallel processing, such as small database instances, caching layers, and lightweight text processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized instance with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 9B14 CPU, offering strong single-core performance for memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-4 features 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, optimized for memory-intensive, single-core workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio for memory-bound workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor with 4 vCPUs and 32 GB RAM.
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks.
Supports AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and cryptographic hardware acceleration.
Configured without local storage or bundled GPU resources.
Best suited for small databases, caching, and lightweight text processing.
